_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Evaluate EAs at the same skill level or below against JQR/JQS line items.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in building and developing high-performing data and AI teams, including talent acquisition, onboarding design, performance management, career development planning, and succession preparation.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-S-006 (LS, cosine: 0.413), AI-T-013 (AI, cosine: 0.389).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.1/10 (raw 0.306). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. DCWF-only terms [against, eas, evaluate, items, jqr, jqs] vs WIDAI-only terms [acquisition, ai, building, career, data, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.829 (P=0.833, R=0.825). Balanced P/R — the 13-word DCWF element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (95.7%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Evaluate EAs at the same skill level or below against JQR/JQS line items." and "Skill in building and developing high-performing data and AI teams, including ta". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.434 > STS 0.306 (gap +0.128). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 2.9%. Shared tokens: [skill]. DCWF-only: [against, eas, evaluate, items, jqr, jqs]. WIDAI-only: [acquisition, ai, building, career, data, design].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 95.7% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.128 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
